description: Main geological definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: geology
words.description: study of the composition, structure, physical properties, and history of Earth's components, and the processes by which they are shaped

words.name: mineralogy
words.description: scientific study of minerals

words.name: petrology
words.description: branch of geology that studies the origin, composition, distribution and structure of rocks

words.name: geomorphology
words.description: scientific study of landforms and the processes that shape them

words.name: sedimentology
words.description: study of natural sediments and of the processes by which they are formed

words.name: stratigraphy
words.description: study of rock layers and the layering process

words.name: volcanology
words.description: field of geology dedicated to the research of volcanoes

words.name: seismology
words.description: scientific study of earthquakes

words.name: geochemistry
words.description: science that applies chemistry to geological systems

words.name: geophysics
words.description: study of physical processes and phenomena occurring in the Earth and in its vicinity, and of their characteristics

words.name: hydrogeology
words.description: branch of science that studies distribution and movement of groundwater

words.name: paleontology
words.description: scientific study of the past of life on Earth through fossils

words.name: geochronology
words.description: science of determining the age of rocks, sediments and fossils

words.name: glaciology
words.description: study of glaciers

words.name: mineral
words.description: naturally occurring usually inorganic substance that has a (more or less) definite chemical composition and a crystal structure

words.name: crystal
words.description: solid material whose constituent atoms, molecules, or ions are arranged in an ordered lattice extending in all three spatial dimensions

words.name: quartz
words.description: mineral made of silicon and oxygen

words.name: feldspar
words.description: mineral group

words.name: mica
words.description: group of phyllosilicate minerals

words.name: calcite
words.description: carbonate mineral

words.name: olivine
words.description: solid solution series

words.name: pyroxene
words.description: group of inosilicate minerals with single chains of silica tetrahedra

words.name: amphibole
words.description: any of a group of double chain inosilicates (mineralogy)

words.name: hardness
words.description: measure of how resistant solid matter is to various kinds of permanent shape change when a force is applied

words.name: cleavage
words.description: tendency of crystalline materials to split along definite crystallographic structural planes

words.name: lustre
words.description: manner in which light interacts with a crystal, rock, or mineral's surface

words.name: streak
words.description: color of the powder produced when a mineral specimen is dragged across an un-weathered surface

words.name: ore
words.description: rock containing one or more valuable minerals, mined for metals but also non-metals that can be extracted from the minerals; metal ores usually contain the metals as parts of compounds with other elements

words.name: rock
words.description: naturally occurring solid aggregate of one or more minerals or mineraloids

words.name: magma
words.description: mixture of molten or semi-molten rock, volatiles and solids that is found beneath the surface of the Earth

words.name: lava
words.description: molten rock expelled by a volcano during an eruption

words.name: granite
words.description: common type of intrusive, felsic, igneous rock with grained texture

words.name: basalt
words.description: mafic igneous rock

words.name: limestone
words.description: sedimentary rocks made of the chemical substance calcium carbonate

words.name: sandstone
words.description: type of sedimentary rock

words.name: shale
words.description: fine-grained, clastic sedimentary rock

words.name: marble
words.description: non-foliated metamorphic rock commonly used for sculpture and as a building material

words.name: gneiss
words.description: common and widely distributed type of rock

words.name: schist
words.description: medium-grade metamorphic rock

words.name: slate
words.description: fine-grained, foliated, homogeneous, low-grade metamorphic rock

words.name: metamorphism
words.description: change of minerals in rocks without melting into magma, occurring due to heat, pressure, and chemically active fluids

words.name: crystallization
words.description: process by which a solid with a highly organised atomic or molecular structure forms

words.name: fault
words.description: fracture or discontinuity in rock across which there is displacement

words.name: fold
words.description: geological structure

words.name: anticline
words.description: in geology, an anticline is a type of fold that is an arch-like shape

words.name: syncline
words.description: type of geological fold

words.name: weathering
words.description: breaking down of rocks, soil and minerals as well as artificial materials through contact with the Earth's atmosphere, biota and waters

words.name: erosion
words.description: action of exogenic processes which remove soil and rock from one location on the Earth's crust, then transport it to another location where it is deposited

words.name: deposition
words.description: geological process in which sediments, soil and rocks are added to a landform or land mass

words.name: landslide
words.description: type of natural disasters involving ground movements, often caused by slope instability triggered by specific event

words.name: soil
words.description: natural body consisting of layers that are primarily composed of minerals

words.name: sediment
words.description: particulate matter that is deposited on the surface of land

words.name: sedimentation
words.description: depositing / withdrawal of particles from fluids or gases under the influence of gravity or the centrifugal force

words.name: lithification
words.description: geologic process

words.name: compaction
words.description: geological process by which a sediment progressively loses its porosity due to the effects of pressure from loading

words.name: cementation
words.description: term in geology

words.name: stratum
words.description: layer of sedimentary rock or soil with internally consistent characteristics

words.name: unconformity
words.description: distorted sediment deposition

words.name: volcano
words.description: natural, surface vent or fissure usually in a mountainous form

words.name: caldera
words.description: cauldron-like volcanic feature usually formed by the collapse of land following a volcanic eruption

words.name: hotspot
words.description: volcanic region

words.name: earthquake
words.description: result of a sudden release of energy in the Earth's crust that creates seismic waves

words.name: epicenter
words.description: point on the Earth's surface that is directly above the hypocenter in an earthquake

words.name: hypocenter
words.description: site of an earthquake or a nuclear explosion

words.name: tsunami
words.description: series of water waves caused by the displacement of a large volume of a body of water

words.name: crust
words.description: outermost solid shell of a rocky planet, dwarf planet or natural satellite, which is chemically distinct from the underlying mantle

words.name: mantle
words.description: layer inside a planetary body

words.name: lithosphere
words.description: rigid, outermost shell of a terrestrial-type planet or natural satellite that is defined by its rigid mechanical properties

words.name: asthenosphere
words.description: highly viscous, mechanically weak and ductile region of the Earth's mantle

words.name: isostasy
words.description: state of gravitational equilibrium between Earth's crust and mantle

words.name: subduction
words.description: geological process at tectonic plate boundaries

words.name: groundwater
words.description: water located beneath the ground surface

words.name: aquifer
words.description: permeable rock containing groundwater

words.name: porosity
words.description: ratio of void volume and total volume of a porous material

words.name: discharge
words.description: volume of fluid which passes per time

words.name: fossil
words.description: preserved remains or traces of organisms from a past geological age

words.name: glacier
words.description: large persistent body of ice

words.name: moraine
words.description: glacially formed accumulation of unconsolidated glacial debris

words.name: coal
words.description: combustible sedimentary rock composed primarily of carbon

words.name: petroleum
words.description: naturally occurring flammable liquid

triples.name: structural geology
triples.description: science of the description and interpretation of deformation in the earth's crust independent of extent

triples.name: economic geology
triples.description: branch of geology concerning the scientific study of the formation, location, and use of marketable geologic materials including fuels, metals, minerals, and water.

triples.name: silicate mineral
triples.description: rock-forming minerals with predominantly silicate anions

triples.name: Mohs scale
triples.description: qualitative ordinal scale characterizing scratch resistance of various minerals

triples.name: specific gravity
triples.description: ratio of the density (mass per volume) of a substance to the density of a given reference material

triples.name: igneous rock
triples.description: one of the three main rock types; formed through the cooling and solidification of magma or lava

triples.name: sedimentary rock
triples.description: rock formed by the deposition of material

triples.name: metamorphic rock
triples.description: rock arising from metamorphism of original rock under high heat and pressure

triples.name: rock cycle
triples.description: time-consuming transitions through geologic time among the three main rock types: sedimentary, metamorphic, and igneous
terms used to denote the orientation of a geologic feature

triples.name: transform fault
triples.description: plate boundary where the motion is predominantly horizontal

triples.name: geologic time scale
triples.description: a system of chronological dating that classifies geological strata (stratigraphy) in time

triples.name: volcanic eruption
triples.description: natural phenomenon where lava, hot gases or other substances are emitted suddenly from a geological feature

triples.name: magma chamber
triples.description: underground accumulation of molten rock

triples.name: seismic wave
triples.description: seismic, volcanic, or explosive energy that travels through Earth's layers

triples.name: P wave
triples.description: longitudinal seismic wave

triples.name: S wave
triples.description: transverse seismic wave

triples.name: moment magnitude
triples.description: a measure of the size of an earthquake based on the seismic moment released

triples.name: moment magnitude scale
triples.description: measure of earthquake size, in terms of the energy released

triples.name: Richter scale
triples.description: measuring the strength ("size") of earthquakes

triples.name: seismic moment
triples.description: product of the shear modulus, area of rupture along the fault, and the average slip

triples.name: oceanic crust
triples.description: part of Earth's lithosphere

triples.name: continental crust
triples.description: layer of rocks that forms the continents and continental shelves

triples.name: inner core
triples.description: innermost geologic layer of the Earth

triples.name: outer core
triples.description: fluid layer composed of mostly iron and nickel between Earth's solid inner core and its mantle

triples.name: geothermal gradient
triples.description: variation of temperature of Earth at different depths

triples.name: plate tectonics
triples.description: scientific theory that describes the large-scale motions of Earth's lithosphere

triples.name: continental drift
triples.description: movement of Earth's continents relative to each other

triples.name: seafloor spreading
triples.description: process at mid-ocean ridges, where new oceanic crust is formed through volcanic activity and then gradually moves away from the ridge

triples.name: convergent boundary
triples.description: area where two tectonic plate collide

triples.name: divergent boundary
triples.description: linear feature that exists between two tectonic plates that are moving away from each other

triples.name: bulk modulus
triples.description: measure of how incompressible / resistant to compressibility a substance is

triples.name: shear modulus
triples.description: ratio of shear stress to the shear strain

triples.name: water table
triples.description: top of a saturated aquifer, or where the water pressure head is equal to the atmospheric pressure

triples.name: permeability (geology)
triples.description: measure of the ability of a porous material to allow fluids to pass through it

triples.name: hydraulic conductivity
triples.description: measure of the ability of a porous material to allow water to pass through it

triples.name: hydraulic head
triples.description: specific measurement of liquid pressure above a vertical datum

triples.name: radiometric dating
triples.description: a technique used to date materials such as rocks or carbon

triples.name: fossil fuel
triples.description: combustible fuel containing hydrocarbons formed by natural processes such as anaerobic decomposition of buried dead organisms, examples being coal, petroleum and natural gas

triples.name: natural gas
triples.description: fossil fuel

triples.name: void volume
triples.description: the volume of the void (pore) spaces within a material

triples.name: total volume
triples.description: the total bulk volume of a material including solids and voids
the mass per unit volume of the substance being measured
the mass per unit volume of pure water, about 1000 kg per cubic metre

triples.name: temperature change
triples.description: the difference in temperature between two points

triples.name: depth interval
triples.description: the vertical distance between two points below the surface

triples.name: cross-sectional area
triples.description: the area of a cross-section taken perpendicular to the direction of flow

triples.name: hydraulic gradient
triples.description: the change in hydraulic head per unit distance along the flow path

triples.name: rock density
triples.description: the mass per unit volume of a rock

triples.name: P wave velocity
triples.description: the propagation speed of a P wave through a medium

triples.name: S wave velocity
triples.description: the propagation speed of an S wave through a medium

triples.name: void ratio
triples.description: the ratio of the volume of voids to the volume of solids in a material

triples.name: solid volume
triples.description: the volume occupied by the solid particles of a material

triples.name: head difference
triples.description: the difference in hydraulic head between two points

triples.name: flow length
triples.description: the distance along the flow path between two points

formulas.name: definition of P wave velocity
formulas.expression: "P wave velocity" = sqrt(( "bulk modulus" + ( 4 / 3 ) * "shear modulus" ) / "rock density")

sources.name: geology reference data
sources.description: typical reference values for common rocks and the Earth's interior, as cited in geology textbooks
